description: As a Water Resource Specialist, calculate the total flood storage volume of the White River reach at the peak of the flood event using Python. Using the simulation results in /home/ga/Documents/hec_ras_projects/Muncie/Muncie.p04.hdf, write a script at /home/ga/Documents/analysis_scripts/compute_flood_volume.py that identifies the time step with the maximum total flow area across all cross-sections and computes the total water volume in the reach at that peak using the trapezoidal rule: V = Sum((A_i + A_{i+1})/2 * L_i), where L_i is the channel length between sections. Convert the result from cubic feet to cubic meters (1 ft^3 = 0.0283168 m^3). Generate a report at /home/ga/Documents/hec_ras_results/flood_volume_report.txt containing the HDF5 filename, the peak time step index, the cross-section count, and the calculated volume in both cubic feet and cubic meters.
